summary: This bundle records deterministic supporting evidence that representative Cautilus readable views are generated from machine-readable packets and preserve packet-first truth boundaries.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: representative report, review, compare, scenario, evidence-bundle, run-index, claim-status Markdown, and claim-status browser review surfaces
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: The tested surfaces cover representative shipped packet-backed renderer families and claim-status readable surfaces. JSON packets remain the audit source for these surfaces; Markdown and HTML are readable projections or review shells over those packets. This bundle is not sufficient by itself to satisfy the broad reviewable-artifacts user-facing claims.
notClaimed: Every future Cautilus renderer family is covered automatically
Readable views prove behavior by themselves
HTML views are the source of truth
Every possible packet field is shown in every readable view
Consumer-owned custom report views follow this contract without their own tests

protectedBehaviors: report, review, review-summary, compare, scenario proposal, conversation review, evidence bundle, and run-index HTML renderers read JSON packets and reject wrong schema versions where applicable
HTML write helpers render next to the JSON input instead of replacing the packet
artifact links rewrite JSON or spec markdown references to adjacent HTML views while retaining source labels
run-index discovery recognizes known packet files and flags whether an HTML projection exists
claim status Markdown is a human-readable projection over the selected claim packet, status summary, review results, validation reports, eval plans, refresh plans, and canonical map
the status report tells readers to use JSON packets as the audit source
browser review renders the Markdown report for human decisions without mutating the source report
browser comments are saved as a separate JSON packet with a report fingerprint
stale saved comments are ignored when the report fingerprint changes

decision.reason: Focused Go and Node tests prove projection behavior for representative readable views, but broad reviewable-artifacts claims still need an aggregate invariant or decomposition by artifact family before they can be marked satisfied.
